title: Smoky Light Egyptian Chicken Kebabs
description: A post-workout Egyptian dish featuring smoky flavors, serrano ham, and tadka, suitable for a sugar-free diet. This recipe combines the flavors of Egypt with the spiciness of serrano ham and the crunch of vegetables.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together tadka spice blend, smoked paprika, olive oil, and lemon juice.
Add the chicken, serrano ham, red onion, red bell pepper, and zucchini to the bowl and toss to coat.
Thread the chicken and vegetables onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat leftovers to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) before serving.
